new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, other than sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10, that is either put up in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, or otherwise put up with an excess pressure due to dissolved carbon dioxide of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar, measured at a temperature of 20 °C.

new_classes.label_en: mushroom stopper packaging (with tie or fastening)
new_classes.definition_en: Packaging (typically a bottle) closed with a mushroom-shaped stopper held in place by ties or fastenings, as used for certain semi-sparkling wines classified under CN 2204 21 06 to 2204 21 09.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the two main distinguishing criteria for this group (CN 2204 21 06–09): (1) wine of fresh grapes other than heading 2204 10 sparkling wine, (2) excess pressure ≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar due to dissolved CO₂ at 20 °C (modelled via ExcessPressureQuality with decimalRange facets), and (3) the mushroom-stopper bottle presentation (new MushroomStopperPackaging class). The container volume constraint (≤ 2 L, since this is subheading 2204 21) is captured by reusing the existing SmallContainer class. However, CN 22042109 is the residual 'other' subheading within the 22042106–09 group, meaning it covers wines in that pressure range NOT falling under 22042106/07/08 (e.g. specific PDO/geographical distinctions among those codes); these further discriminating sub-criteria (e.g. specific PDO status, specific wine types like Crémant) cannot be fully modelled without additional legal text for each sibling subheading. The complement restriction on SparklingWine is used as a proxy to exclude heading 2204 10 wines, though a cleaner disjointness axiom would require the full sibling structure. Overall coverage of the primary physical and chemical distinguishing criteria is good but the 'other within the group' residuality is not fully formalised.
